description_long: This object is a traditional garden pitchfork with a long, straight wooden handle and a metal head featuring eight robust, evenly spaced tines. The handle is cylindrical, tapering slightly towards the top, and appears to be made of varnished or aged wood, giving it a natural brown hue. The head is constructed from dark, possibly steel or iron, metal, and is securely fastened to the handle with a reinforced collar. Each tine is thick and squared, designed for durability and effective soil or debris movement. The overall construction suggests it is built for heavy-duty use in urban gardens, parks, or maintenance areas. There are no visible signs of rust or significant wear, and the grip end of the handle is bare, without any additional covering.
description: A classic pitchfork with a long wooden handle and a sturdy metal head featuring eight thick, evenly spaced tines. The tool is robust and well-suited for garden or park maintenance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the metal head and all eight tines clearly aligned.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the tines and the depth of the metal head.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the back of the tines and the handle's attachment.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the profile of the tines and the straightness of the handle.
